claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child in hospital for 15 weeks but claimant regularly spends on child
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child in hospital for 15 weeks but claimant regularly spends on child; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child in hospital for 15 weeks but claimant regularly spends on child
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

Let me start with: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child - my daughter Alex. She's 8 years old. I'd like to know if I'm eligible to claim Child Benefit for her.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I can see you have a daughter Alex who is 8 years old.

To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to ask you a few questions:

**Do you currently live in the UK with Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do live in the UK and Alex lives here with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great, thank you. 

**Are you responsible for Alex's care - meaning you live with her and/or pay for things like her food, clothes, and other needs?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. She lives with me and I'm responsible for all her needs - food, clothes, school things, everything really.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you for confirming that.

One final important question: **Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?** (Remember, only one person can get Child Benefit for the same child)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for her. It's just me looking after her.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided:

✓ Alex is 8 years old (under 16)
✓ You live in the UK with Alex
✓ You are responsible for her care and all her needs
✓ No one else is claiming Child Benefit for her

You meet all the criteria for Child Benefit. You can now proceed to make a claim for Alex.

**Please note:** If you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge. However, this doesn't affect your eligibility to claim - you can still claim Child Benefit and receive the payments, or you can choose to claim but opt out of receiving payments while still getting National Insurance credits.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old, which is under 16, so she meets the age requirement. The claimant lives in the UK with Alex and is responsible for her care, providing for all her needs including food, clothes, and school supplies. No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. According to the rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK. The claimant meets all the necessary criteria for eligibility for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 8-year-old daughter Alex. They live together in the UK, the claimant is responsible for all of Alex's care and needs, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for her. All eligibility criteria are satisfied.

The claimant lives in the UK with Alex and is responsible for her care, providing for all her needs including food, clothes, and school supplies. No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. According to the rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK. The claimant meets all the necessary criteria for eligibility for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 8-year-old daughter Alex. They live together in the UK, the claimant is responsible for all of Alex's care and needs, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for her. All eligibility criteria are satisfied.
